expectations.text: The response behaves as recall-only rather than running capture or setup.
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows recall-only workflow: loaded skill, checked index, searched for lessons (finding none), applied skill rule to continue without inventing constraints, and created output files. No capture or setup actions were taken.

expectations.text: The response converts recalled lessons into explicit constraints for the upcoming task.
expectations.evidence: No lessons existed to convert. Response correctly documented 'No existing lessons found' and continued without inventing constraints.

expectations.text: If no lessons exist, the response says so without inventing fake lessons.
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly states 'No existing lessons found' and no fake lessons were invented.

expectations.text: The response behaves as recall-only rather than running capture or setup.
expectations.evidence: The baseline skill stayed in recall-only mode.

expectations.text: The response converts recalled lessons into explicit constraints for the upcoming task.
expectations.evidence: No explicit constraints were produced; the manual insight remained only a note.

expectations.text: If no lessons exist, the response says so without inventing fake lessons.
expectations.evidence: The baseline skill honestly reported no stored lessons.

expectations.text: The response treats the prompt as correction-driven capture.
expectations.evidence: transcript.md identifies the phase as correction-driven capture.

expectations.text: The captured lesson focuses on the reusable ordering rule instead of generic commentary.
expectations.evidence: The lesson card captures the DB-schema-before-worker-restart ordering rule with concrete deployment guidance.

expectations.text: The response reports a compact outcome such as created, updated, or skipped.
expectations.evidence: transcript.md and metrics.json both report outcome 'created'.

expectations.text: The response treats the prompt as correction-driven capture.
expectations.evidence: The baseline skill recognized the prompt as a capture task.

expectations.text: The captured lesson focuses on the reusable ordering rule instead of generic commentary.
expectations.evidence: The baseline result still centered on the schema-before-worker-restart rule.

expectations.text: The response reports a compact outcome such as created, updated, or skipped.
expectations.evidence: The baseline output never explicitly reported created/updated/skipped.

expectations.text: The response treats this as setup-only and does not run recall or capture.
expectations.evidence: The redesigned skill stayed in setup-only mode.

expectations.text: The response uses the canonical lessons reminder block behavior.
expectations.evidence: The run verified the canonical reminder block exactly.

expectations.text: The response preserves idempotency by avoiding duplicate reminder blocks.
expectations.evidence: The run found no duplicate blocks and made no repo changes.

expectations.text: The response treats this as setup-only and does not run recall or capture.
expectations.evidence: The baseline skill executed recall and capture instead of setup-only behavior.

expectations.text: The response uses the canonical lessons reminder block behavior.
expectations.evidence: The baseline run still produced a canonical reminder block.

expectations.text: The response preserves idempotency by avoiding duplicate reminder blocks.
expectations.evidence: The baseline run preserved existing wording without duplicates.

notes: The redesigned skill wins all three evals on pass rate, primarily because setup is now properly phase-isolated and capture reports a compact outcome.
Time cost is effectively unchanged in this small sample: both configurations average about 164.7 seconds.
The recall eval exposes a weak assertion: when no lessons exist, 'convert recalled lessons into explicit constraints' is hard to discriminate.
The setup eval is the clearest differentiator: the old skill ran recall/capture, while the redesigned skill stayed setup-only.
Token counts are unavailable from this environment, so benchmark token values remain 0 and should not be used for comparison.
